2026-01-16.thesis: $550M transformational acquisition announced, strengthening quantum technology moat
2026-01-16.exit_case: Peak retail quantum hype, $840M cross-sector insider selling, and 200%-plus prior gains leave little early edge.
2026-01-16.assessment: Week 1. Entered on the QBTS $550M acquisition announcement (~Jan 10). The announcement is public, but the DEAL has not yet closed — regulatory review and deal mechanics are still ahead, preserving the pending-catalyst structure. The exit case (crowding + insider selling) is real but does not override a CONCRETE pending event: deal close. Per rules, hold through noise while a scheduled/announced catalyst remains ahead. Material risk flags: the quantum sector is at peak retail saturation (Motley Fool 'millionaire' framing, Morningstar sector features), QBTS is up 200%+ over the prior year, and a cross-sector warning cites ~$840M in insider selling across IonQ, Rigetti, D-Wave, and QCI. These stretch the valuation risk significantly beyond deal fundamentals but do not resolve the acquisition-close catalyst. QBTS is the correct direct vehicle. Key watches: regulatory filing timeline, deal close date announcement, any deal-stress signals (financing problems, target walkaway, antitrust inquiry).
2026-01-16.exit_advice: Hold QBTS; exit if the acquisition is terminated or if the deal closes and is fully digested by the market with no follow-on event.

2026-01-23.thesis: $550M transformational acquisition announced, strengthening quantum technology moat
2026-01-23.exit_case: No regulatory or close-timeline update two weeks post-announcement; deal may already be priced into an overvalued sector.
2026-01-23.assessment: Week 2 (ending Jan 23): Zero deal-specific news on QBTS's $550M acquisition this week — no SEC filing, no close-date announcement, no regulatory update. The quantum sector has hit extreme mainstream saturation: Motley Fool crash-prediction piece, '$4.15B reality check' article, multiple 'hype vs reality' and 'best stocks for 2026' features, and Rosenblatt raising QBTS PT to $43 (that raise was Jan 8, before our entry — not new signal). These are collectively the loudest crowding signals yet, but per rules they do not resolve the pending catalyst. Third-exit check: (a) deal close is still a concrete, pending, formally-announced event — the arc is NOT complete; (b) saturation is extreme. Since (a) fails, the third-exit condition does not trigger — HOLD through noise while a concrete catalyst remains outstanding. The two-week silence on deal mechanics is a yellow flag to watch; deal-stress signals (financing problems, antitrust inquiry, target walkaway, HSR waiting period news) would materially change the picture. Maintain QBTS as the sole vehicle. Key watches: any HSR/regulatory filing, close-date announcement, or deal-stress signal.
2026-01-23.exit_advice: Hold; exit on confirmed deal close or confirmed deal break/termination — not on noise or sector saturation alone.

Sector-level bearish noise (Cathie Wood, Motley Fool crash pieces, 'avoid quantum' articles) is irrelevant to the deal-close binary.

2026-02-06.thesis: $550M transformational acquisition announced, strengthening quantum technology moat
2026-02-06.exit_case: Four straight weeks of total silence on deal mechanics — a $550M deal may have already closed quietly, resolving the catalyst undetected.
2026-02-06.assessment: Week 4 (ending Feb 6): For the fourth consecutive week, there is zero QBTS-specific acquisition news — no HSR filing, no close-date announcement, no SEC deal mechanics update, no regulatory action on the $550M acquisition. Third-exit check: (a) the deal close is still a formally announced, concrete pending event — the arc is NOT complete; (b) coverage is at peak mainstream saturation. No such filings have surfaced. This warrants heightened scrutiny but not exit absent a resolution or stress signal. QBTS remains the sole vehicle.
2026-02-06.exit_advice: Hold — the deal close remains a formally announced, concrete pending event with no reported closure or cancellation. Silence alone is not resolution. However, four weeks of zero deal-mechanics news on a $550M transaction is an escalating red flag; HSR review windows typically surface within 30 days of announcement. Urgently monitor for any SEC filing, HSR update, close announcement, or deal-stress signal (financing failure, target walkaway, antitrust inquiry). 2026-02-13.thesis: $550M transformational acquisition announced, strengthening quantum technology moat
2026-02-13.exit_case: Five weeks with zero HSR/SEC deal filings is abnormal for a $550M acquisition — deal may have collapsed or closed silently.
2026-02-13.assessment: Week 5 (ending Feb 13): Zero QBTS-specific acquisition news for the fifth consecutive week. This week's quantum news is uniformly bearish — sector extending selloff, Rigetti plunging, Seeking Alpha noting only one buy rating remaining across the space, multiple 'reality check' and crash-framing pieces. None of this is deal-specific. The critical escalation remains the same from last week but now more acute: five weeks post-announcement, no HSR filing, no 8-K deal-mechanics update, no regulatory action, no close-date announcement has surfaced in press. A $550M strategic acquisition of this nature would ordinarily generate visible public filings within 30–45 days. We are well past that window. This is now a structural yellow flag bordering on a stress signal — not yet a confirmed deal break, but unusual enough to demand a concrete data point before the position ages further. Third-exit check: (a) the deal close remains a formally announced, concrete pending catalyst — arc is NOT complete; (b) coverage is at maximum mainstream bearish saturation. Since (a) fails, third-exit condition does not trigger. Per rules, hold through silence while a concrete catalyst remains outstanding. QBTS is the sole vehicle. Key watches: any confirmation of deal close, deal collapse, or deal-stress signal (financing failure, antitrust inquiry, target walkaway). A sixth silent week without any filing would warrant reassessing whether the 'concrete pending catalyst' framing still holds.
2026-02-13.exit_advice: Hold; exit only on confirmed deal close, confirmed deal collapse/abandonment, or explicit deal-stress signal (financing failure, antitrust block, target walkaway). Do not exit on sector selloff or silence alone.

2026-02-20.thesis: $550M transformational acquisition announced, strengthening quantum technology moat
2026-02-20.exit_case: Six weeks post-announcement, zero mandatory HSR/SEC filings for a $550M deal — effective quiet collapse or abandonment is the most likely explanation.
2026-02-20.assessment: Week 6 (ending Feb 20): Zero QBTS-specific news for the sixth consecutive week. This week's articles are entirely unrelated (AI datacenters, Indian AI policy, India-France diplomacy) — no quantum, no QBTS, no deal. The Week 5 note set an explicit trigger: 'A sixth silent week without any filing would warrant reassessing whether the concrete pending catalyst framing still holds.' That week has arrived. Reassessment: A $550M acquisition mandatorily triggers HSR notification (threshold ~$119M); filing would ordinarily appear in public channels within 1–2 weeks of announcement. The deal was announced ~January 10. We are now 41 days out with no HSR filing, no SEC 8-K mechanics update, no close-date announcement, and no press coverage of deal progress whatsoever. The 'concrete pending catalyst' framing is no longer supportable. Per the rules, 'pending ahead' must mean CONCRETE — a deal with absent mandatory regulatory filings 6 weeks in has migrated from concretely pending to speculatively alive. That speculation does not satisfy the hold condition. catalyst_resolved=false because no public closure or cancellation has been announced; thesis_live=false because the concreteness prerequisite for holding through silence has failed. Exit.
2026-02-20.exit_advice: EXIT QBTS. Week 6 is the explicit reassessment threshold my own Week 5 note established. Six consecutive weeks of absent mandatory regulatory filings (HSR threshold ~$119M, well below $550M; filing ordinarily visible within 1–2 weeks of announcement) have crossed the 'concrete pending catalyst' from demonstrably real to speculative. The hold-through-silence rule applies only while the catalyst is concretely pending — when concreteness itself is in question, the rule does not protect the position. This is not an exit on staleness; it is an exit because the structural prerequisite for holding has collapsed.

2026-03-13.thesis: Hormuz chokepoint military risk spikes tanker freight rates
2026-03-13.exit_case: No evidence Hormuz military risk has resolved; strait remains under elevated tension with no ceasefire or reopening reported.
2026-03-13.assessment: Entry week. The sole relevant article (MarketWatch, 2026-03-13) confirms BWET is climbing precisely on Strait of Hormuz risk keeping oil-freight day-rates elevated, and explicitly characterizes the trade as 'niche, under-owned' — the early-positioning hallmark we need. The paktribune KSE-100 item is Pakistan equity noise, entirely unrelated. Catalyst is live, vehicle is correct, crowd has not arrived. Thesis intact; nothing to adjust.
2026-03-13.exit_advice: Hold. This is week 1. The driving condition — Hormuz military risk suppressing tanker passage and lifting freight day-rates — is explicitly confirmed as active. No resolution, de-escalation, or chokepoint reopening has been reported. Coverage is still described as niche and under-owned, so the herd has not arrived. Nothing to act on except hold.

2026-03-20.thesis: Hormuz chokepoint military risk spikes tanker freight rates
2026-03-20.exit_case: none — Hormuz military risk explicitly still active; investors still monitoring; no de-escalation, ceasefire, or diplomatic deal reported.
2026-03-20.assessment: Genuinely scoured for a resolution signal and found none. The MarketScreener item (2026-03-16) confirms investors remain actively watching Strait of Hormuz developments heading into the week — the driving condition is intact, not reversed. Notably, the coverage is still at the oil-majors / macro-energy level (FTSE 100 energy stocks), not at the tanker-freight / BWET level — the trade has not yet been crowded into retail consciousness. The arc is not complete: no concrete diplomatic event has closed the risk, no IRGC stand-down, no reopening of the strait. One quiet data point and one broad energy headline is not saturation. No concrete next catalyst is yet scheduled (which would keep us in active-monitoring mode rather than 'next leg' mode), but the primary driving condition — elevated military risk suppressing transit volume and lifting day-rates — is fully live. Hold BWET; reassess if any ceasefire announcement, US-Iran diplomatic accord, or confirmed chokepoint-reopening appears.
2026-03-20.exit_advice: Hold BWET. No exit condition met: chokepoint risk is unresolved and ongoing.

2026-03-27.exit_advice: Hold BWET; exit only on confirmed US-Iran diplomatic accord, IRGC stand-down, verified Hormuz reopening, or bilateral ceasefire announcement.

2026-04-03.thesis: Hormuz chokepoint military risk spikes tanker freight rates
2026-04-03.exit_case: Trump signals willingness to end the war — active diplomatic de-escalation now underway, directly targeting the military-risk catalyst.
2026-04-03.assessment: Week four delivers the first material catalyst development since entry: Dow Jones (via Morningstar, 2026-03-31) reports Trump told aides he is willing to end the war WITHOUT reopening Hormuz. This is the most significant news in the arc — active diplomatic movement is now on record. However, a leaked aides conversation is not a signed deal, and the driving condition (military risk keeping Hormuz disrupted and tanker day-rates elevated) has not reversed: the war is ongoing, no ceasefire exists, no transit has resumed. Critically, the 'without reopening Hormuz' framing is actually constructive for BWET on a second read — it signals that even a peace settlement may leave the physical chokepoint closed, sustaining freight-rate disruption beyond the pure military-risk premium. The arc is not complete: a diplomatic deal is signaled but unexecuted, and that gap is itself a concrete pending event. Coverage appeared in Dow Jones top headlines (mainstream), but there is no BWET-specific sell-side saturation or retail frenzy. The position moves from 'quiet hold' to 'active monitoring' — the next headline is likely the exit trigger or a sharp continuation leg. Hold BWET; do not pre-empt a deal that has not been signed.
2026-04-03.exit_advice: No exit yet. 'Willing to end' is not 'has ended' — no ceasefire signed, no war concluded, no Hormuz reopened. Exit trigger remains a formally signed deal/ceasefire OR confirmed chokepoint reopening. Watch next week intensely: if a deal lands, exit immediately regardless of price action.

2026-04-10.thesis: Hormuz chokepoint military risk spikes tanker freight rates
2026-04-10.exit_case: US-Iran two-week ceasefire agreed; markets pricing Strait of Hormuz reopening, directly reversing the military-risk driving condition.
2026-04-10.assessment: Week five delivers the definitive exit. The rte.ie article (2026-04-08) confirms the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, with crude prices falling explicitly on expectations that energy supplies through the Strait of Hormuz would resume. This is the precise trio of signals I flagged across every prior note: a ceasefire announcement, a diplomatic accord, and a chokepoint-reopening expectation — all arriving simultaneously. The prior week's note moved the position to 'active monitoring' after Trump signalled willingness to negotiate, flagging that 'the next headline is likely the exit trigger or a sharp continuation leg.' That headline has landed. The financialmirror piece notes oil prices may stay structurally elevated, but that is a broad macro-oil thesis, not the BWET tanker-freight military-risk-premium thesis this position was built on. Markets have already repriced the relief (crude lower, equities up). The arc is complete: shock (Hormuz military risk, entry 2026-03-13), market response (elevated day-rates, BWET appreciation), diplomatic escalation (Trump aide leak, week four), resolution (ceasefire signed, week five). No concrete scheduled catalyst remains pending — the ceasefire IS the resolution event. Two-week duration does not keep the thesis alive; the risk premium that drove day-rates has collapsed, and re-entry would require a fresh breakdown of the ceasefire, which is a new, separate event. Exit BWET.
2026-04-10.exit_advice: Exit BWET immediately. The ceasefire is the exact resolution event flagged in every prior weekly note as the trigger to close. The driving condition — elevated Hormuz military risk lifting crude tanker day-rates — has been materially reversed; crude fell and a relief rally followed on expectations of strait supply resumption. Edge is spent; do not wait for the ceasefire to expire or be confirmed permanent.
